name: Scaffold a Worker in the User's Language
query: Write me a Python worker that processes the charge_card task
expected_behavior: Step 1: Confirm the language (Python) and the task type name (charge_card)
Step 2: Consult references/workers.md for the Python SDK pattern
Step 3: Generate a Python worker using the `@worker_task` decorator from `conductor.client.worker.worker_task`, decorated with `task_definition_name='charge_card'`
Step 4: Include the TaskHandler boilerplate to actually start the worker (`TaskHandler(configuration=config, scan_for_annotated_workers=True)`)
Step 5: Show the install command: `pip install conductor-python`
Step 6: Add a comment or docstring noting the worker must be idempotent (Conductor may redeliver on failure or timeout)
Step 7: Mention the worker gate — the task type name in the worker must match the `name` of the SIMPLE task in the workflow definition exactly
Step 8: Offer to verify against an existing workflow JSON if the user has one
success_criteria: Worker uses the `@worker_task` decorator pattern from references/workers.md (not a hand-rolled poll loop)
The `task_definition_name` argument exactly matches the user-supplied task name `charge_card`
TaskHandler / runner code is included so the worker is actually runnable, not just a function
Install command (`pip install conductor-python`) is shown
Idempotency note is present as a code comment or docstring
Agent does not invent a different SDK or library name (e.g. it does not write `pip install conductor` or `from conductor import ...`)
